card.intuition: A graph is bipartite iff it can be 2-coloured: assign alternating colours during BFS/DFS. If you ever find two adjacent nodes of the same colour, a cycle of odd length exists — the graph is not bipartite.
card.analogy: Checking if a sports league can split all teams into two groups where every game is played between groups (no intra-group games). Assign each team a colour; if two rival teams end up the same colour, the schedule is impossible.
when_to_use: Checking if a graph can be 2-coloured (scheduling, matching readiness)
gotchas: A graph is bipartite iff it has no odd-length cycle
Run BFS/DFS from every unvisited node to handle disconnected graphs
The colour assignment itself is the two-partition — useful for follow-up matching algorithms

hints: Assign the opposite colour to v: if color[u] == 0 assign 1, and vice versa — use 1 - color[u]
exercise.test_code:
# Even cycle (bipartite)
adj1 = {0:[1,3], 1:[0,2], 2:[1,3], 3:[2,0]}
assert is_bipartite(4, adj1) == True

# Odd cycle (not bipartite)
adj2 = {0:[1,2], 1:[0,2], 2:[0,1]}
assert is_bipartite(3, adj2) == False

# Disconnected bipartite
adj3 = {0:[1], 1:[0], 2:[3], 3:[2]}
assert is_bipartite(4, adj3) == True

# Single node
assert is_bipartite(1, {0:[]}) == True
print('PASS')
